title: Study aboutthe use of Kabbalah and Esotericism in Evangelion
display_title: Study aboutthe use of Kabbalah and Esotericism in Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: Study aboutthe use of Kabbalah and Esotericism in Evangelion             Quote Post by ido » Thu Sep 15, 2005 7:05 pm Hello Everybody, I am a student writing a seminar paper about the use of Kabbalah and esoteric symbolism in Neon Genesis Evangelion. I read a lot of articles on the net, about Kabbalah in Evangelion, but I am still lacking some information for my research, and was hoping one of you could help me out. Any answers to these questions would be welcome. Answers which will include refernces or links would even be better! 1. Did Hideaki Anno or any of the other creators of Evangelion mention the reason for the use of esoteric and kabbalistic symbolism and terminology in the series, or talking about its meaning? 2. What are the assumed sources which Anno or his crew used, to get the symbolism? Did Anno read any specific Kabbalah books, or study Kabbalah somewhere? Did he have some other people studying that topic? (I heard a rumor that he had Japanese Talmud scholars who designed the kabbalistc references) 3. How was the reaction in Japanese audiences to the use of Kabbalah in the series? 4. Do you know of any other Anime series of films that make use of kabbalistic concepts or symbolism? 5. What context does Japanese culture or Anime culture provide for use of such elements? Is use of esoteric symbolism popular in Japanese Anime? If so, why? Your help is greatly appreciated, Ido Hartogsohn

[]:             Quote Post by TsubasaNoKoikokoro » Thu Sep 15, 2005 8:12 pm 1. Did Hideaki Anno or any of the other creators of Evangelion mention the reason for the use of esoteric and kabbalistic symbolism and terminology in the series, or talking about its meaning? Yes a lot. But the exact meaning of it in the series is beyond me. The tree of Sephiroth is very important though.[.i] 2. What are the assumed sources which Anno or his crew used, to get the symbolism? Did Anno read any specific Kabbalah books, or study Kabbalah somewhere? Did he have some other people studying that topic? (I heard a rumor that he had Japanese Talmud scholars who designed the kabbalistc references) [i]No, Anno admitted himself that there are no Religion in Evangelion but he thought the symbols looked "cool." 3. How was the reaction in Japanese audiences to the use of Kabbalah in the series? As far as I am aware, they didn't really notice anything usual. 4. Do you know of any other Anime series of films that make use of kabbalistic concepts or symbolism? Yes, there are tons that use jewish and christian symbolism. Chrno Crusade to name one. 5. What context does Japanese culture or Anime culture provide for use of such elements? Is use of esoteric symbolism popular in Japanese Anime? If so, why? Well. It is populare for christian symbols to appear in anime because they look cool and very few people are christian in Japan, so it does not offend many people until dubs translation. [/i]

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Thu Sep 15, 2005 10:00 pm The only information about the use of the mythical/religious symbols in Eva I heard from the sources (ie the guys who did Eva) was that they used them so that it would "look cool". If you analyze on their use in Eva you'll probably find that it's use in Eva differs from the original meant use in either the myth or the religion.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Sep 15, 2005 10:13 pm A couple of statements by the creators here: http://www.evacommentary.org/op/op_1.html

[]:             Quote Post by ido » Fri Sep 16, 2005 4:09 am Reichu wrote: A couple of statements by the creators here: http://www.evacommentary.org/op/op_1.html

[]: Re: Study aboutthe use of Kabbalah and Esotericism in Evange             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Sep 16, 2005 1:40 pm ido wrote: 1. Did Hideaki Anno or any of the other creators of Evangelion mention the reason for the use of esoteric and kabbalistic symbolism and terminology in the series, or talking about its meaning?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Sep 16, 2005 5:35 pm ido wrote: great link.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Sep 16, 2005 5:47 pm It should be noted that "lance" is used rather than "spear" in the whole Grail/Joseph of Arimathea/Fisher King side of the Matter of Britain; it's not just an NGE translation usage.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Sep 16, 2005 11:08 pm Mr. Tines wrote: It should be noted that "lance" is used rather than "spear" in the whole Grail/Joseph of Arimathea/Fisher King side of the Matter of Britain; it's not just an NGE translation usage.

[]: To Reichu and Obsessivemathfreak             Quote Post by ido » Sat Sep 17, 2005 9:10 am Thanks a lot for you help. I like reading your views and ideas about these topics, and the list of esoteric refereneces in Evangelion was fantastic. In response to Reichu: Esoteric doesn't mean to me, that the information about esoteric sciences (e.g. Alchemy, Astrology, Kabbalah) is hard to obtain, but that these sciences are a part of a distinct western esotericist tradition. This doesn't contradict the fact that today a lot of esoteric knowledge is made available. However, it is still considered esoteric knowledge from an esoteric tradition. Spear of Longhinus and the Cross aren't dominant at all in Judaism, in fact they are non-existent, as Christ himself is of course absent from this religion. In respons to obsessivemathfreak: I'm afraid you won't be able to read my work, as I am writing it in Hebrew. I study in the Tel Aviv University in Israel, and am writing this specific Seminar paper for a course in Christian Kabballah. My paper which will be about 30-40 pages long, will concentrate on two major topics. 1. The historical/cultural background which leads Kabballah to suddenly appear in a Japanese television show. What is this Kabbalah, and how is it linked to pop culture modern religious movements and so on. Trying to understand which kind of kabbalah this is: Judaic Kabbalah, Christian Kabbalah, Hermetic Kabbalah or New Age Kabbalah. 2. Next I will convey an survey and interpertation on the key kabbalistic ideas and concepts which are to be found in Evangelion and will analyse ideas such as Good and Evil in evangelion, or Cosmological worldview - in kabalistic terms. I will also try to present some basic kabbalistic readings to the whole of Evangelion, but I am sure it would be difficult to reach some one complete and flawless hermeneutic structure. I am not sure what the outcome will be yet, but I think it will probably revolve a lot around the idea that Evangelion presents a new offspring on the historic memetic tree of Kabbalah, which mixes kabbalistic symbolic and ideas together with New Age ideas and concepts (Such as these are presented in book like: New Age Religion and Western Culture, by the Dutch scholar Wouter Hanegraaff). It creates an amalgam of Jungian Psychology, Kabbalah, different New Age ideas or ideas and more... Hope it sounds interesting to you. I believe it will be fun to write, and have been planning the writing for longer than a year now. Also some Professors here have become quite curious about it... Maybe i'll translate parts of the thing for you, if there are any interested readers. Ido,

[]: Re: To Reichu and Obsessivemathfreak             Quote Post by TsubasaNoKoikokoro » Sat Sep 17, 2005 9:16 am ido wrote: 2. Next I will convey an survey and interpertation on the key kabbalistic ideas and concepts which are to be found in Evangelion and will analyse ideas such as Good and Evil in evangelion, or Cosmological worldview - in kabalistic terms. I will also try to present some basic kabbalistic readings to the whole of Evangelion, but I am sure it would be difficult to reach some one complete and flawless hermeneutic structure.

[]: Re: To Reichu and Obsessivemathfreak             Quote Post by ido » Sat Sep 17, 2005 10:34 am Tsu-chan wrote: Well,l first off I'd be intrested in reading.But in Evangelion there is no evil, just people with diffrent opinions, some who think ending the world is good for humans *coughseelecough* and others who don't want it to end.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Sep 17, 2005 11:43 am Is Jahshua [it sounds so much nicer than "Jesus"] altogether absent from Judaism? Somehow, I was under the impression that Jahshua was there, but just not recognized as a part of YHWH (the Trinity being a Christian invention and all). [Speaking of the Trinity, I added that to the list above.] Please correct me if I'm wrong. You know Hebrew? Hey, that gives me an idea... :goes to PM ido:

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Sep 17, 2005 1:21 pm Reichu wrote: Just remembered "red earth (purification ceremony)". Rei makes a reference to red earth in #14, and the purification ceremony is from EoE. Rei is referring to the genesis of humanity (made from dust/earth), although I still don't know where the "red earth purification ceremony" comes from.

[Speaking of the Trinity, I added that to the list above.] Please correct me if I'm wrong. You know Hebrew? Hey, that gives me an idea... :goes to PM ido:

[]:             Quote Post by K2Grey » Sun Sep 18, 2005 12:44 am Ido, this is a very interesting idea, which I haven't heard of before, and I wish you good luck on writing up this paper. Reichu, Adam was supposed to become a giant of light before consuming the Fruit of Knowledge? Huh? Time to try and Google that :)

Article: Study aboutthe use of Kabbalah and Esotericism in Evangelion
 won't be able to read my work, as I am writing it in Hebrew. I study in the Tel Aviv University in Israel, and am writing this specific Seminar paper for a course in Christian Kabballah. My paper which will be about 30-40 pages long, will concentrate on two major topics. 1. The historical/cultural background which leads Kabballah to suddenly appear in a Japanese television show. What is this Kabbalah, and how is it linked to pop culture modern religious movements and so on. Trying to understand which kind of kabbalah this is: Judaic Kabbalah, Christian Kabbalah, Hermetic Kabbalah or New Age Kabbalah. 2. Next I will convey an survey and interpertation on the key kabbalistic ideas and concepts which are to be found in Evangelion and will analyse ideas such as Good and Evil in evangelion, or Cosmological worldview - in kabalistic terms. I will also try to present some basic kabbalistic readings to the whole of Evangelion, but I am sure it would be difficult to reach some one complete and flawless hermeneutic structure. I am not sure what the outcome will be yet, but I think it will probably revolve a lot around the idea that Evangelion presents a new offspring on the historic memetic tree of Kabbalah, which mixes kabbalistic symbolic and ideas together with New Age ideas and concepts (Such as these are presented in book like: New Age Religion and Western Culture, by the Dutch scholar Wouter Hanegraaff). It creates an amalgam of Jungian Psychology, Kabbalah, different New Age ideas or ideas and more... Hope it sounds interesting to you. I believe it will be fun to write, and have been planning the writing for longer than a year now. Also some Professors here have become quite curious about it... Maybe i'll translate parts of the thing for you, if there are any interested readers. Ido,
